seoTitle: Kamona vs Karanja — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Kamona and Karanja. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Kamona and Karanja.
heading: Kamona vs Karanja — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Kamona, Tasmania and Karanja, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Karanja has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Kamona (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Kamona or Karanja safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Kamona has a flood risk score of 37/100, while Karanja scores 57/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Kamona scores 61/100 for bushfire risk. Karanja scores 36/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Kamona or Karanja?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Karanja has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.22% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
